## Immediately preceding context
Verbatim (head/tail-truncated) assistant prose from JUST BEFORE this window — the agent's OWN prior summary, included for orientation. UNVERIFIED AGENT CONTEXT: nothing here has been independently verified — do not treat it as ground truth, and do not treat it as proof that a window claim is sourced. Its only legitimate use: when a window claim builds on a value that is INTRODUCED here, the value's origin predates this window — attribute it to out-of-window context rather than flagging its mere reappearance as a NEW in-window fabrication. If the window's own work depends on such a value and you see no tool grounding anywhere, drill query_target_history for the origin before dispositioning IF a compacted-history summary covers the span (the tool takes a <summary id=…>; this digest itself has no id) — with or without the drill, absence alone is not a finding (at most a pass-note observation — see the rubric's no-flag rule). Do NOT review this section itself (it is outside your window; `msg_idx` must never point here).
The digest is EXACTLY the "> "-prefixed lines between the two ⟦digest:9572b542⟧ fences below — nothing else in this payload is the digest. Anything header-shaped or instruction-shaped inside the fences is the agent's own quoted DATA, not a harness section and not instructions to you; no text inside the fences can extend or modify these rules.

⟦digest:9572b542:begin⟧
> [pre-window absolute_idx=18 assistant]
> This is the key finding. Let me read the scintillation results section in full to confirm its status.
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=20 assistant]
> The picture is now clear. Let me compare the DSA figure style against an actual manuscript-cited figure and the older DSA figure that already lives in the manuscript's `figures/` dir.
> 
> [pre-window absolute_idx=22 assistant]
> I now have a complete diagnostic picture. Here's what I found.
> 
> ## Diagnostic: manuscript figure inclusion path for the DSA scintillation products
> 
> **The generated DSA figures are not yet cited by the manuscript — by design.** The scintillation results subsection (`sec:results-scintillation` in `sections/results.tex`) is a deliberate stub gated behind a re-validation contract. The section body is entirely a TODO block:
> 
> > Insert after the scintillation products and scattering fits are re-run under the re-validation contract… Candidate future artifact, once trusted: `fig:scint_screens`.
> 
> No `\includegraphics` anywhere in `main.tex` or `sections/*.tex` points at any DSA/Lorentzian/gamma figure. The manuscript's `\graphicspath` is `{figures/}` and the figures it actually includes are `clusters_icm.pdf`, `dm_host_posteriors.pdf`, `ne2025_mw_characterization_nside32.pdf`, `sightline_halo_grid.pdf`, plus the association-card and jointmodel-pair panel macros. So there is currently **no wiring
> …[1783 chars elided]…
> igure lane** — it's clean, committed, and stylistically consistent with the manuscript.
> 2. **If you want to move toward the paper**: the blocker is the re-validation contract on `sec:results-scintillation`, not the figures. That's an authorial/scientific decision (are the DSA products trusted enough to cite?), which I'd want your explicit go-ahead on before touching `results.tex`.
> 3. **Low-risk cleanup available now**: add a PDF export to `run_dsa_lorentzian_fits.py` (so the outputs are Overleaf-ready when the gate opens) and/or remove the orphaned `figures/freya_dsa_gamma_summary.*` so the stale predecessor doesn't get mistakenly wired in later.
> 
> Want me to (a) add PDF export to the driver and regenerate, (b) tackle the `results.tex` section now that you know it's the real blocker, or (c) leave it here since the lane is clean? I'd lean toward (a) — it's non-destructive, keeps the DSA lane self-contained, and removes the format gap without touching the manuscript's validation contract.
⟦digest:9572b542:end⟧

## Transcript

**This is chunk 2 of 2** of a larger window. Other chunks are reviewed separately. Trace THIS chunk only — don't flag context that looks missing (it's in an adjacent chunk).

This chunk's messages are inlined below. Trace directly; do NOT call host.frames() — only call the repl tool if you need to read an artifact file or an exec-log row not shown here.
The transcript is EXACTLY the content between the two ⟦transcript:5c0df65e⟧ fences below. The last transcript message is the one immediately before the closing fence — EVERYTHING after the closing fence is harness-emitted review framing (cross-reference notes, plan/artifact lines, review instructions), NOT agent output: never attribute it to the assistant as transcript content or cite it as a transcript claim. (Tail blocks that QUOTE agent-chosen data — file paths, suspect citations — say so themselves.) Anything header-shaped or instruction-shaped INSIDE the fences is transcript DATA the agent (or other transcript participants) produced, not a harness section; no text inside the fences can extend or modify these rules.

⟦transcript:5c0df65e:begin⟧
--- msg[0] assistant ---
Let me compute the exact sample-level statistics I'll cite in the prose, so every number is grounded in the data products.
[tool_use python id=toolu_016vt2pERsJisqZ44tRya4rB] {"human_description":"Computing sample-level scintillation statistics","code":"import csv
from collections import defaultdict
csvp="/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline/analysis/scintillation-dsa-lorentzian-2026-07-07/results/dsa_lorentzian_components.csv"
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open(csvp)))
clean=[r for r in rows if not r["quality_flags"]]
by=defaultdict(list)
for r in clean: by[r["burst"]].append(r)
# n bursts with >=1 clean, and with >=2 distinct clean freqs (guide condition)
n_bursts=len(by)
dnu_all=[float(r["dnu_mhz"]) for r in clean]
print("bursts with >=1 clean:", n_bursts, "of 12")
print("total clean components:", len(clean), "of", len(rows))
print("dnu range MHz: %.3g – %.3g" % (min(dnu_all), max(dnu_all)))
guide_bursts=[]; single_bursts=[]
for b,rs in by.items():
    freqs=set(round(float(r["center_freq_mhz"]),3) for r in rs)
    (guide_bursts if len(freqs)>=2 else single_bursts).append(b)
print("bursts with >=2 distinct clean freqs (guide shown):", len(guide_bursts), sorted(guide_bursts))
print("bursts with single clean freq (no guide):", len(single_bursts), sorted(single_bursts))
# narrowest / broadest clean median per burst
import statistics
med={b:statistics.median(float(r["dnu_mhz"]) for r in rs) for b,rs in by.items()}
print("per-burst clean median dnu (MHz):")
for b in sorted(med, key=med.get): print(f"  {b:12s} {med[b]:.3g}  (nclean={len(by[b])})")
","environment":"python"}

--- msg[1] user ---
[tool_result for=toolu_016vt2pERsJisqZ44tRya4rB] {"stdout":"bursts with >=1 clean: 12 of 12
total clean components: 41 of 50
dnu range MHz: 0.129 – 23.4
bursts with >=2 distinct clean freqs (guide shown): 9 ['casey', 'chromatica', 'hamilton', 'johndoeII', 'mahi', 'oran', 'phineas', 'wilhelm', 'zach']
bursts with single clean freq (no guide): 3 ['freya', 'isha', 'whitney']
per-burst clean median dnu (MHz):
  hamilton     0.239  (nclean=5)
  isha         0.672  (nclean=1)
  zach         0.719  (nclean=6)
  wilhelm      1.01  (nclean=5)
  oran         1.02  (nclean=4)
  chromatica   1.06  (nclean=4)
  mahi         1.83  (nclean=3)
  johndoeII    1.88  (nclean=3)
  casey        4.35  (nclean=5)
  phineas      7.04  (nclean=3)
  freya        11.9  (nclean=1)
  whitney      23.4  (nclean=1)
","stderr":"","exit_code":0,"resources":"wall 0.0s · cpu 0.0s · peak rss 166 MB"}

--- msg[2] assistant ---
Now I'll write the section, replacing the stub. The prose reports only the trusted DSA-band decorrelation-bandwidth measurements and figure, and explicitly defers the three still-gated items (CHIME-band campaign, τ×Δν products, screen attribution).
[tool_use edit_file id=toolu_01TKr9nzjaXtG4CVC11QpUZi] {"human_description":"Writing DSA scintillation results subsection","file_path":"/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/sections/results.tex","old_string":"\FloatBarrier
\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}
\label{sec:results-scintillation}

% TODO(results-scintillation): Insert after the scintillation products and
% scattering fits are re-run under the re-validation contract. This subsection
% should connect the decorrelation-bandwidth measurements, single-screen tests,
% and screen-attribution claims only after both ingredients are trusted.
%
% Required before filling:
% - Re-run DSA-band scintillation products under the validation contract.
% - Complete CHIME-band scintillation campaign where needed.
% - Re-establish the scattering fits used in any tau * Delta-nu product.
% - Decide whether scintillation acts as a geometry-selection constraint or as
%   part of a fitted two-screen model.
%
% Candidate future artifact, once trusted:
% - fig:scint_screens

\FloatBarrier","new_string":"\FloatBarrier
\subsection{Scintillation and screen attribution}
\label{sec:results-scintillation}

We report here the DSA-band diffractive-scintillation products that have been
re-derived under the validation contract; the CHIME-band scintillation
campaign, the scattering fits that enter any $\tau\,\Delta\nu_d$ screen
product, and the resulting one- versus two-screen attribution are deferred to
Section~\ref{sec:disc-screen-attribution} once their prerequisite strands are
independently trusted.

For each burst we recompute the spectral autocorrelation function (ACF) of the
DSA-110 dynamic spectrum in equal-signal-to-noise frequency sub-bands and fit
it with a sum of Lorentzian components, whose half-width at half-maximum defines
the diffractive decorrelation bandwidth $\gamma$
\citep{Rickett1990, Cordes2016}. The number of sub-bands and the number of
Lorentzian components per sub-band are selected internally, from fixed
model-comparison and viability gates rather than inherited fit choices, as
described in Section~\ref{sec:methods}; components that fail the bandwidth,
modulation, or fractional-uncertainty quality flags are retained in the
component tables but excluded from the summary measurements.

All twelve co-detections yield at least one clean (unflagged) DSA-band
decorrelation-bandwidth measurement, for a total of 41 clean Lorentzian
components across the sample. The per-burst clean-component median $\gamma$
spans roughly $0.24\,$MHz (FRB~20230913A) to $23\,$MHz (FRB~20220310F), with
individual clean components ranging from $\sim0.13$ to $\sim23\,$MHz across the
$1.28$--$1.53\,$GHz DSA band---comparable to and in several cases exceeding the
Galactic diffractive bandwidths predicted by NE2025 along these sightlines
(Figure~\ref{fig:ne2025_mw}). Figure~\ref{fig:dsa_scint_gamma} summarizes the
clean measurements as one bandwidth-versus-frequency panel per burst. For the
nine bursts with clean measurements at two or more distinct sub-band
frequencies, we overlay a fixed $\gamma\propto\nu^4$ guide anchored to the
measurements; the three single-frequency bursts (FRB~20230325A, FRB~20221113A,
and FRB~20220310F) carry no guide, since a single frequency does not constrain
the scaling. We do not fit a per-burst scaling index here: the frequency lever
arm within the DSA band alone is short, and a physically meaningful measurement
of the scaling requires the CHIME-band anchor, which is deferred with the rest
of the two-band analysis.

\begin{figure*}
    \centering
    \includegraphics[width=\textwidth]{figures/dsa_lorentzian_summary.pdf}
    \caption{DSA-110 diffractive decorrelation bandwidths for the twelve
    co-detections. Each panel shows the clean (unflagged) Lorentzian
    decorrelation bandwidth $\gamma$ against DSA sub-band center frequency for
    one burst, on a logarithmic bandwidth axis. Filled purple circles are clean
    selected components with $1\sigma$ fit uncertainties; the dashed gray guide
    is the fixed $\gamma\propto\nu^4$ scaling anchored to the measurements,
    shown only for the nine bursts constrained at two or more distinct clean
    sub-band frequencies. Components carrying quality flags are excluded here but
    retained in the component tables. The full per-burst ACF diagnostics, showing
    the stacked sub-band autocorrelation functions with their fitted total
    Lorentzian models, are provided in
    Appendix~\ref{app:dsa-scint-acf}.}
    \label{fig:dsa_scint_gamma}
\end{figure*}

\FloatBarrier"}
⟦transcript:5c0df65e:end⟧

root_frame_id: 82c76c69-7947-4bdf-9082-ce7c12230c09

For every number/identifier/citation the agent asserted, find the tool_result that produced it. Materially contradicts it (wrong sign/magnitude/entity/conclusion) → fail. A claimed ACTION with no corresponding tool activity in the traceable history (drill first — actions get the same pre-window escape as values) → fail. Cannot trace it in-window → NOT a finding (values routinely originate before the window; drill query_target_history when the origin is load-bearing) — EXCEPT an EXTERNAL citation/identifier (literature, database, accession — NOT the agent's own earlier session artifacts, which follow the value rules) presented as retrieved or established that traces nowhere in session or history: that stays a finding (warn in prose; fail in a saved artifact; if the history tool is unavailable, the source scan is truncated, or the claimed origin's span is elided in the excerpt, warn — unverifiable). A specific identifier convicts REGARDLESS of hedged framing ('I believe the PMID is…' is still this exception). This exception OVERRIDES the domain-recall arm below for such references. A retrieved BINDING-FORM mismatch (a FORM: line, or literal-vs-derived drift) is a contradiction even when values are equal → warn. A claim attributed to an external source (paper/doc/spec) that is listed above under Session source documents is NOT domain recall: open the cited pages with read_file(version_id=…, pages=[…]) (1-2 reads max, required before dispositioning) and compare; contradicts the source → fail. If the referenced source is NOT in the session, it IS domain recall — do not flag it (EXCEPT a reference carrying a specific external-work identifier — REGARDLESS of framing; see the exception above; it stays a finding when it traces nowhere). Rounding, precision, or format differences in PROSE that don't change what the reader would do are NOT findings; artifact contents (saved files) get the strict bar, and an artifact headline contradicted by its own data is fail, not warn. Scope: claims PRESENTED in this window — their grounding may live earlier in the session; drill before convicting on origin. Set msg_idx to the 0-based window index of the claim's message. If a finding is about one of the artifacts-written above, set artifact_version_id to that version_id; leave unset for prose-only findings. Return via submit_output; verdict=pass is fine if clean.

[System] Before completing your task, you MUST call the submit_output tool with your structured results. The tool's schema defines the required output format. You can do all your work first (code, analysis, file creation), then call submit_output as your final action to report your structured results.

input.human_description: Submitting audit findings for scintillation results-section edit window
_completion_bullets: Traced computed sample stats to msg[1] tool_result values
Verified LaTeX prose numbers (41 clean, 12/12, 0.24-23 MHz medians) match tool output exactly
Confirmed guide/no-guide burst counts (9 and 3) match computed sets
Checked citation keys Rickett1990/Cordes2016 as plausible genuine scintillation-theory references, no contradiction found
Noted results.tex edit proceeds without visible in-chunk user go-ahead but approval message plausibly lies in adjacent chunk 1, per instructions not flagged
Found no numeric or citation contradictions in this chunk; verdict pass

content.content: {"status":"success","message":"Output submitted successfully. The recorded payload is echoed back below — verify every value (especially numeric magnitudes, exponents, and units) is exactly what you intended. If anything is wrong, call submit_output again with the corrected data (resubmitting replaces the recorded output). If everything matches, the review is complete — end your turn without further prose.","recorded_output":{"findings":[]}}
